definition:
ζόφος, -ου, ὁ
 (akin to γνόφος, which see), [in Sm.: Exo.10:22, Job.28:3, Psa.11:2 91:6, Isa.59:9 * ;] 
in Hom, the gloom of the under-world; hence, darkness, deep gloom (poët. and late prose writers): Heb.12:18, 2Pe.2:4 2:17, Ju 6 13.†
 ὁμίχλη, -ης, ἡ, 
 [in LXX for עֲרָפֶל, etc. ;] 
a mist: 2Pe.2:17 
 SYN.: νέφος, νεφέλη, both thicker than ὁ  (AS)
